description: §45 — the JavaScript strict-equality operator `===` is NOT a valid scrml operator and SHALL fire E-EQ-004. `if (a === b)` is rejected; scrml has a single equality operator `==` (already strict, with type-matching). Reject-path (pos) half of the E-EQ-004 pin; clean neg is `equality/equality-operator-clean` (the valid `==` form → silent). (`!==` is the sibling trigger, recovering to `!=`.)
rationale: §45: scrml equality is always strict, so there is one operator `==` (and `!=`); the `===`/`!==` triple-equals forms are not valid scrml and are E-EQ-004 (recovers to `==`/`!=`).
